docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Welcome to the FrangipaneTeam Terraform Framework Validators documentation site This repository contains a collections of validators for the Terraform Plugin Framework.

docs.title: Validators
docs.text:
• StringValidator
  • BoolValidator
  • Int64Validator
  • ListValidator
  • SetValidator
  • MapValidator

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
For installing the validators, you can use the go get command:

go get github.com/FrangipaneTeam/terraform-plugin-framework-validators@latest

docs.title: BoolValidator
docs.text: Bool validator are used to validate the plan of a boolean attribute. I will be used into the Validators field of the schema.BoolAttribute struct.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
import (
fboolvalidator "github.com/FrangipaneTeam/terraform-plugin-framework-validators/boolvalidator"
)

docs.title: Special
docs.text: • Not - This validator is used to negate the result of another validator.

docs.title: Not
docs.text: This validator is used to check if the validators passed as arguments are NOT met.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
// Schema defines the schema for the resource.
func (r *xResource) Schema(ctx context.Context, _ resource.SchemaRequest, resp *resource.SchemaResponse) {
resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
(...)
"enabled": schema.StringAttribute{
Optional:            true,
MarkdownDescription: "Enable ...",
Validators: []validator.String{
fstringvalidator.Not(fstringvalidator.IsValidIP())
},
},

docs.title: Int64Validator
docs.text: Int64 validator are used to validate the plan of a int64 attribute. I will be used into the Validators field of the schema.Int64Attribute struct.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
import (
fint64validator "github.com/FrangipaneTeam/terraform-plugin-framework-validators/int64validator"
)

docs.title: Special
docs.text: • Not - This validator is used to negate the result of another validator.

docs.title: Not
docs.text: This validator is used to check if the validators passed as arguments are NOT met.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
// Schema defines the schema for the resource.
func (r *xResource) Schema(ctx context.Context, _ resource.SchemaRequest, resp *resource.SchemaResponse) {
resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
(...)
"count": schema.Int64Attribute{
Optional:            true,
MarkdownDescription: "Count of ...",
Validators: []validator.Int64{
fint64validator.Not(int64validator.Between(10,20))
},
},

docs.title: Not
docs.text: This validator is used to check if the validators passed as arguments are NOT met.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:

resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
(...)
"list_of": schema.ListAttribute{
Optional:            true,
MarkdownDescription: "List of ...",
Validators: []validator.List{
flistvalidator.Not(listvalidator.Between(10,20))
},
},

docs.title: StringValidator
docs.text: String validator are used to validate the plan of a string attribute. I will be used into the Validators field of the schema.StringAttribute struct.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
import (
fstringvalidator "github.com/FrangipaneTeam/terraform-plugin-framework-validators/stringvalidator"
)

docs.title: Network
docs.text:
• IsValidIP - This validator is used to check if the string is a valid IP address.
  • IsValidNetmask - This validator is used to check if the string is a valid netmask.

docs.title: String
docs.text:
• IsValidURN - This validator is used to check if the string is a valid URN.
  • IsValidUUID - This validator is used to check if the string is a valid UUID.

docs.title: Special
docs.text: • Not - This validator is used to negate the result of another validator.

docs.title: IsValidIP
docs.text: This validator is used to check if the string is a valid IP address.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
// Schema defines the schema for the resource.
func (r *xResource) Schema(ctx context.Context, _ resource.SchemaRequest, resp *resource.SchemaResponse) {
resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
(...)
"ip_address": schema.StringAttribute{
Optional:            true,
MarkdownDescription: "IPV4 for ...",
Validators: []validator.String{
fstringvalidator.IsValidIP()
},
},

docs.title: IsValidNetmask
docs.text: This validator is used to check if the string is a valid netmask.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
// Schema defines the schema for the resource.
func (r *xResource) Schema(ctx context.Context, _ resource.SchemaRequest, resp *resource.SchemaResponse) {
resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
(...)
"netmask": schema.StringAttribute{
Optional:            true,
MarkdownDescription: "Netmask for ...",
Validators: []validator.String{
fstringvalidator.IsValidNetmask()
},
},

docs.title: IsValidURN
docs.text: This validator is used to check if the string is a valid URN.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
// Schema defines the schema for the resource.
func (r *xResource) Schema(ctx context.Context, _ resource.SchemaRequest, resp *resource.SchemaResponse) {
resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
(...)
"vm_id": schema.StringAttribute{
Optional:            true,
MarkdownDescription: "The VM ID for ...",
Validators: []validator.String{
fstringvalidator.IsValidURN()
},
},

docs.title: IsValidUUID
docs.text: This validator is used to check if the string is a valid (v4) UUID.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
// Schema defines the schema for the resource.
func (r *xResource) Schema(ctx context.Context, _ resource.SchemaRequest, resp *resource.SchemaResponse) {
resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
(...)
"vm_id": schema.StringAttribute{
Optional:            true,
MarkdownDescription: "The VM ID for ...",
Validators: []validator.String{
fstringvalidator.IsValidUUID()
},
},

docs.title: Not
docs.text: This validator is used to check if the validators passed as arguments are NOT met.

docs.title: How to use it
docs.text:
// Schema defines the schema for the resource.
func (r *xResource) Schema(ctx context.Context, _ resource.SchemaRequest, resp *resource.SchemaResponse) {
resp.Schema = schema.Schema{
(...)
"ip_address": schema.StringAttribute{
Optional:            true,
MarkdownDescription: "IPV4 for ...",
Validators: []validator.String{
fstringvalidator.Not(fstringvalidator.IsValidIP())
},
},
